text.title: Architecture Repo Scaffolding Template Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the architecture-repo-scaffolding-template methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Repo MUST contain `adr/`, `diagrams/`, `fitness/`, `runbooks/`, `reviews/` top-level dirs; renaming is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Uniform paths let tooling and reviewers find evidence without per-repo navigation.

rule.statement: `reviews/cadence.yml` MUST declare next-review date, owner, and evidence sources before merge.
rule.rationale: Without a pinned cadence the repo decays in <2 quarters and ADRs become unreliable.

rule.statement: Every fitness function in `fitness/` MUST be executable code (CI-runnable), not prose.
rule.rationale: Prose fitness functions are not measurable and silently rot.

rule.statement: `adr/template.md` MUST follow MADR or Nygard shape; no free-form ADRs.
rule.rationale: Free-form ADRs lose machine-parseable consequence-evidence binding.

rule.statement: `README.md` MUST list each dir with one-line purpose + link to canonical methodology.
rule.rationale: Without root routing readers cannot navigate the repo in <60s.

rule.statement: Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
